message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  COVID-19 has become a global pandemic that threatens millions of people worldwide. There is an urgent call for developing effective drugs against the virus (SARS-CoV-2) causing this disease. The main protease of SARS-CoV-2, 3C-like protease (3CL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  ), is highly conserved across coronaviruses and is essential for the maturation process of viral polyprotein. Scutellariae radix (Huangqin in Chinese), the root of
                  <jats:italic>Scutellaria baicalensis</jats:italic>
                  has been widely used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat viral infection related symptoms. The extracts of
                  <jats:italic>S. baicalensis</jats:italic>
                  have exhibited broad spectrum antiviral activities. We studied the anti-SARS-CoV-2 activity of
                  <jats:italic>S. baicalensis</jats:italic>
                  and its ingredient compounds. We found that the ethanol extract of
                  <jats:italic>S. baicalensis</jats:italic>
                  inhibits SARS-CoV-2 3CL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  activity
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  and the replication of SARS-CoV-2 in Vero cells with an EC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  of 0.74 μg/ml. Among the major components of
                  <jats:italic>S. baicalensis</jats:italic>
                  , baicalein strongly inhibits SARS-CoV-2 3CL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  activity with an IC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  of 0.39 μM. We further identified four baicalein analogue compounds from other herbs that inhibit SARS-CoV-2 3CL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  activity at microM concentration. Our study demonstrates that the extract of
                  <jats:italic>S. baicalensis</jats:italic>
                  has effective anti-SARS-CoV-2 activity and baicalein and analogue compounds are strong SARS-CoV-2 3CL
                  <jats:sup>pro</jats:sup>
                  inhibitors.
                </jats:p>
title: Scutellaria baicalensis extract and baicalein inhibit replication of SARS-CoV-2 and its 3C-like protease in vitro
